mission.title: Install and Configure Amazon Elastic Kubernetes Service (EKS)
mission.description: Amazon EKS Anywhere allows you to create and manage Kubernetes clusters on your own infrastructure, providing a consistent experience similar to Amazon EKS. It is ideal for organizations that want to run Kubernetes on-premises while maintaining AWS management capabilities. Key trade-offs include the need for local infrastructure and potential complexity in managing on-prem resources.

steps.title: Install the EKS Anywhere CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the EKS Anywhere command-line interface, which is required to create and manage your clusters.

```bash

curl -LO https://github.com/aws/eks-anywhere/releases/download/v0.25.0/eks-a-linux-amd64

chmod +x eks-a-linux-amd64

sudo mv eks-a-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/eksctl

```

> Download the EKS Anywhere CLI for Linux.
> Make the downloaded binary executable.
> Move the binary to a directory in your PATH.

steps.title: Create an EKS Anywhere Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a new EKS Anywhere cluster with the specified Kubernetes version and node count.

```bash

eksctl anywhere create cluster --name my-cluster --kubernetes-version 1.31 --node-count 3 --region us-west-2

```

> Replace 'my-cluster' with your desired cluster name.
commands: eksctl anywhere create cluster --name my-cluster --kubernetes-version 1.31 --node-count 3 --region us-west-2

steps.title: Configure kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step sets up your kubeconfig file to interact with your new EKS Anywhere cluster.

```bash

aws eks update-kubeconfig --name my-cluster --region us-west-2

```

> Ensure AWS CLI is configured with appropriate permissions.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This step verifies that the nodes in your cluster are up and running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see your nodes listed with a 'Ready' status.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step configures autoscaling and monitoring for your cluster.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/aws/eks-anywhere/main/examples/autoscaler.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/aws/eks-anywhere/main/examples/monitoring.yaml

```

> Deploy the Cluster Autoscaler.
> Deploy monitoring tools.

uninstall.title: Uninstall EKS Anywhere Cluster
uninstall.description:
This step removes the EKS Anywhere cluster and may result in data loss. Ensure to backup any important data before proceeding.

```bash

eksctl anywhere delete cluster --name my-cluster

```

> This will delete the cluster and all associated resources.

uninstall.title: Cleanup Orphaned Resources
uninstall.description:
This step ensures that any orphaned resources are cleaned up after cluster deletion.

```bash

aws ec2 describe-instances --filters 'Name=tag:eks:cluster-name,Values=my-cluster' --query 'Reservations[*].Instances[*].InstanceId' --output text | xargs aws ec2 terminate-instances

```

> Terminate any orphaned EC2 instances.
commands: aws ec2 describe-instances --filters 'Name=tag:eks:cluster-name,Values=my-cluster' --query 'Reservations[*].Instances[*].InstanceId' --output text | xargs aws ec2 terminate-instances

upgrade.title: Upgrade EKS Anywhere Cluster
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you backup your cluster data. This step will upgrade your cluster to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get nodes -o wide

eksctl anywhere upgrade cluster --name my-cluster --kubernetes-version 1.31

kubectl get nodes

```

> Check the current node status before upgrading.
> Upgrade the cluster to the specified version.
> Verify that nodes are upgraded and in 'Ready' state.

troubleshooting.title: Error: Unable to connect to the server: x509: certificate signed by unknown authority
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This error indicates that the kubeconfig file is not correctly configured.

**Fix:**
Regenerate the kubeconfig file using 'aws eks update-kubeconfig --name my-cluster --region us-west-2'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: node not found
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the nodes are not registered with the cluster.

**Fix:**
Check the node status using 'kubectl get nodes' and ensure they are running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: Insufficient permissions to perform this action
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The IAM role associated with your AWS CLI may not have the necessary permissions.

**Fix:**
Ensure your IAM role has the required EKS permissions.

troubleshooting.title: Cluster Autoscaler not scaling
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Cluster Autoscaler may not be configured correctly.

**Fix:**
Check the autoscaler logs using 'kubectl logs -f deployment/cluster-autoscaler -n kube-system'.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Kubernetes 1.31, improved cluster management features.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the nodes listed with 'kubectl get nodes' showing 'Ready' status, indicating that your EKS Anywhere cluster is operational.
codeSnippets: eksctl anywhere create cluster --name my-cluster --kubernetes-version 1.31 --node-count 3 --region us-west-2

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before starting the installation.
